name: trtllm_fp4_block_scale_routed_moe_topk2_e8_h2048_i1024
description: TensorRT-LLM FP4 block scale MoE with pre-computed routing (routed variant). MxFP4 weights and BF16 activations. Accepts packed topk_ids instead of raw routing logits. Targets SM100 (Blackwell) GPUs.

top_k.description: Number of top experts selected per token.

gemm1_out_size.description: Output size of the first GEMM (gate + up projections). Should be 2 * intermediate_size.

sf_vec_size.description: Block size for MxFP4 scale factors (one scale per 32 elements).

num_hidden_sf_blocks.description: Number of scale factor blocks along the hidden_size dimension (hidden_size / sf_vec_size = 2048 / 32).

num_intermediate_sf_blocks.description: Number of scale factor blocks along the intermediate_size dimension (intermediate_size / sf_vec_size = 1024 / 32).

num_gemm1_sf_blocks.description: Number of scale factor blocks along the gemm1_out_size dimension (gemm1_out_size / sf_vec_size = 2048 / 32).

topk_ids.description: Pre-computed packed routing tensor. Each int32 encodes: bits[31:16]=expert_index, bits[15:0]=expert_weight (bfloat16 bits). Routing must be performed externally before calling this kernel.

gemm1_weights.description: First GEMM weights for all experts (gate and up projections), in float32 for reference. Kernel uses MxFP4-quantized and shuffled weights.

gemm2_weights.description: Second GEMM weights for all experts (down projection), in float32 for reference. Kernel uses MxFP4-quantized and shuffled weights.

reference:
import torch


@torch.no_grad()
def run(
    topk_ids: torch.Tensor,
    hidden_states: torch.Tensor,
    gemm1_weights: torch.Tensor,
    gemm2_weights: torch.Tensor,
):
    """
    Reference implementation for TensorRT-LLM FP4 block scale routed MoE.

    • Routing: pre-computed (topk_ids are packed int32 with expert_idx<<16 | weight_bf16)
    • Activation: SwiGLU (silu(gate) * act)
    • Weights: float32 for reference (kernel uses MxFP4-quantized + shuffled weights)
    • Hidden states: bfloat16 input

    Fixed geometry:
        E=8 experts, H=2048, I=1024, TOP_K=2
    """

    E = 8
    H = 2048
    I = 1024
    TOP_K = 2

    T = topk_ids.shape[0]
    device = topk_ids.device

    assert topk_ids.shape == (T, TOP_K)
    assert hidden_states.shape == (T, H)
    assert gemm1_weights.shape == (E, 2 * I, H)
    assert gemm2_weights.shape == (E, H, I)

    # Unpack topk_ids: bits[31:16]=expert_idx, bits[15:0]=weight_bf16_bits
    expert_idx = (topk_ids >> 16).to(torch.int32)                    # [T, TOP_K]
    weight_bits = (topk_ids & 0xFFFF).to(torch.int16)                # [T, TOP_K] int16
    expert_weights = weight_bits.view(torch.bfloat16).to(torch.float32)  # [T, TOP_K]

    A = hidden_states.to(torch.float32)      # [T, H]
    W13 = gemm1_weights.to(torch.float32)    # [E, 2I, H]
    W2 = gemm2_weights.to(torch.float32)     # [E, H, I]

    output = torch.zeros((T, H), dtype=torch.float32, device=device)

    for e in range(E):
        # Find tokens and their expert weights for expert e
        tok_k_mask = (expert_idx == e)          # [T, TOP_K] bool
        tok_has_e = tok_k_mask.any(dim=1)       # [T] bool
        if not tok_has_e.any():
            continue

        tok_idx = torch.nonzero(tok_has_e, as_tuple=False).squeeze(1)  # [Tk]
        A_e = A[tok_idx]                         # [Tk, H]
        W13_e = W13[e]                           # [2I, H]
        W2_e = W2[e]                             # [H, I]

        # GEMM1: [Tk, H] @ [H, 2I] = [Tk, 2I]
        g1 = A_e @ W13_e.t()

        # SwiGLU: act=first half, gate=second half
        act = g1[:, :I]
        gate = g1[:, I:]
        c = torch.nn.functional.silu(gate) * act   # [Tk, I]

        # GEMM2: [Tk, I] @ [I, H] = [Tk, H]
        o = c @ W2_e.t()

        # Get per-token weights for this expert (sum over the k dimension)
        # Each token selects expert e at most once
        w_tok = (expert_weights[tok_idx] * tok_k_mask[tok_idx].float()).sum(dim=1)  # [Tk]
        output.index_add_(0, tok_idx, o * w_tok.unsqueeze(1))

    return output.to(torch.bfloat16)
